video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance says he believes negotiations with Iran will be "positive." Boarding Air Force Two on his way to Pakistan, Vance added: “If they’re gonna try and play us, then they’re gonna find that the negotiating team is not that receptive.”

video:video.video:description: The largest monthly jump in gas prices in six decades caused a sharp spike in inflation in March, heightening the political challenges for the White House.

video:video.video:description: Dozens of residents in one of Rio de Janeiro’s largest favela gathered to follow a Good Friday procession through the narrow streets of Complexo do Alemão.

video:video.video:description: Chile’s largest private zoo marked Easter Sunday by having several of its animals search for their food during an annual event that resembles an easter egg hunt and is meant to entertain visitors and stimulate the natural behaviors of various species.

video:video.video:title: Trump offered few new details on military operation against Iran in address to nation
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said U.S. forces will keep hitting Iran “very hard” in the next two or three weeks and bring the country “back to the Stone Ages,” even as he touted the success of U.S. operations and argued that all of Washington’s objectives have so far been met or exceeded.

video:video.video:title: Iran says it has accepted a two-week ceasefire in the war
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump pulled back on his threats to launch devastating strikes on Iran late Tuesday, swerving to deescalate the war less than two hours before the deadline he set for Tehran to capitulate or else a “whole civilization will die.”

video:video.video:title: Celebrations in Baghdad as Iraq's soccer team qualifies for 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of people celebrated in Bagdad on Saturday as the Iraqi national soccer team returned to the capital following their qualification for the 2026 World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Route 66, a quintessential American road trip heavy on kitsch and history, turns 100
video:video.video:description: Route 66 marks its 100th anniversary this year. Despite losing its status decades ago as one of the nation’s main arteries, people around the world still flock to it to take the quintessential American road trip (AP Video: Ty ONeil, Julio Cortez, Susan Montoya Bryan, Jae Hong)

video:video.video:title: Traffic through Strait of Hormuz as Iran closes key waterway
video:video.video:description: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli attacks on Lebanon, Iranian state media reported.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers lose hope for humpback whale stranded in Baltic Sea
video:video.video:description: Rescuers said Wednesday that they have given up hope for the humpback whale that has become stranded repeatedly off Germany’s Baltic Sea coast and now expect it to die in the inlet where it currently lies.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts reach orbit on historic mission to the moon and back
video:video.video:description: Four astronauts embarked on a high-stakes flight around the moon Wednesday, humanity’s first lunar voyage in more than half a century and the thrilling leadoff in NASA’s push toward a landing in two years.

video:video.video:title: Trump tells countries that rely on oil from Strait of Hormuz 'they must grab it and cherish it'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is seeking to allay fears in the U.S. about rising gas prices as the war on Iran has continues.

video:video.video:title: Police block protesters marching toward Venezuela’s presidential palace
video:video.video:description: The demonstrators were trying to reach the building in Caracas to demand action on the country’s political prisoners.

video:video.video:title: Hungarian opposition leader Magyar celebrates election win in Budapest
video:video.video:description: In a speech to tens of thousands of jubilant supporters at a victory party along the Danube River on Sunday in Budapest, Péter Magyar said his voters had rewritten Hungarian history.

video:video.video:title: Video brings new scrutiny to ICE shooting in Minneapolis after charges against 2 men are dropped
video:video.video:description: Federal authorities initially accused two men of beating an ICE officer with a broom handle and a snow shovel before opening an investigation into the officers giving untruthful testimony under oath.

video:video.video:title: Kiss rocker Gene Simmons visits Detroit Rock City to open new eatery
video:video.video:description: Gene Simmons visited Detroit Rock City to celebrate the grand opening of a restaurant co-founded by him and his Kiss bandmate Paul Stanley (April 9 - AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi lands in Pyongyang for two-day North Korea visit
video:video.video:description: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi arrived in North Korea on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Attorneys, more family arrive to court hearing for Gilgo Beach serial killier
video:video.video:description: Rex Heuermann accused in a string of long-unsolved slayings known as the Gilgo Beach killings is expected to plead guilty, closing a case that bedeviled investigators, agonized victims’ relatives and tantalized a true-crime obsessed public for years.

video:video.video:title: Brenda Song's Disney Channel 'Mount Rushmore'
video:video.video:description: Brenda Song shares her Disney Channel “Mount Rushmore” while promoting season 2 of her latest project, “Running Point.”

video:video.video:title: Pope in Good Friday Way of the Cross ceremony at Rome's Colosseum
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV personally carried the wooden cross through all 14 stations of the Way of the Cross at the Colosseum on his first Good Friday as pontiff, marking the first time in decades that a pope carries the cross to every station.

video:video.video:title: Africa gears up to welcome the pope
video:video.video:description: Four countries in Africa have begun preparations Friday to welcome Pope Leo XIV, who is set to visit Algeria, Angola, Cameroon and Equatorial Guinea.

video:video.video:description: As night fell over a nature park on the edge of Kyiv in Ukraine, children crowded around volunteers who carefully opened cloth bags and released bats into the twilight.

video:video.video:description: James McAvoy shares what happened when he lied for an audition. His directorial debut "California Schemin'" will premiere in theaters in the U.K. on April 10.

video:video.video:description: American woodcocks came to New York City looking to strut their stuff, and New Yorkers fell in love. (AP video: Emily Wang Fujiyama, Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:description: Philadelphia Flyers fans took a unique approach to support their team by gifting Pope Leo XIV a customized Flyers jersey during a family trip to Italy.

video:video.video:description: Iran, the United States and Israel agreed Tuesday to a two-week ceasefire, an eleventh-hour deal that headed off U.S.

video:video.video:title: See thousands of decorated eggs representing Christ's resurrection ahead of Orthodox Easter
video:video.video:description: As Orthodox Easter approaches, Romania is keeping the tradition of decorated Easter eggs alive with two museums dedicated to the craft.

video:video.video:title: Donald Trump Jr. criticizes the European Union during a trip to Bosnia
video:video.video:description: Donald Trump Jr. lashed out at the European Union on Tuesday, saying its liberal policies were discouraging investment and predicted a “major fracture” between the bloc's eastern and western member states.

video:video.video:title: Surprise! Zendaya wears something blue -- after the old, new and borrowed
video:video.video:description: First she wore something old. Then new, then borrowed — and now blue. Zendaya showed up at her New York premiere for “The Drama” in a dazzling blue Schiaparelli couture ball gown, completing a bridal tease she'd begun weeks ago.

video:video.video:title: Wife of Gilgo Beach murder suspect urges focus on victims, asks for privacy
video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect has pleaded guilty to murdering seven women and admitted he killed an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, April 7th: Trump warns ‘whole civilization will die tonight’ if Iran deal isn't reached; Vance visits Hungary in support of Orbán; gunmen attack building housing Israeli Consulate in Istanbul; Brazil's Indigenous leaders take to the streets.

video:video.video:title: University research center in Tehran severely damaged in airstrike
video:video.video:description: A laser and plasma research center in Tehran was severely damaged following an airstrike Friday. Iran's Minister of Science, Research and Technology, Hossein Simaei Sarraf, accused those who carried out the strike of "Stone Age" behavior.

video:video.video:title: Like her 'Something Bad' character, Camila Morrone listens to her gut
video:video.video:description: Camila Morrone trusts her instincts when making big decisions. She stars in the Netflix horror series "Something Very Bad is Going to Happen."

video:video.video:title: Shopkeepers worried about impact of 'smart lockdown' over fuel price rises
video:video.video:description: Pakistan’s federal government has ordered markets and shopping malls to close in the country by 8:00 p.m., as a fuel crisis deepens in the region.

video:video.video:title: Asia markets react to the failure of U.S.-Iran talks
video:video.video:description: Trading in Asia was weighed down Monday by the failure of the United States and Iran to reach a peace agreement. (AP Video: Johnson Lai and Yong Jun Chang)

video:video.video:title: Tehran residents react to peace talks ending without agreement
video:video.video:description: Some Tehran residents on Sunday were in favor of diplomacy despite high-level talks with the United States ending without an agreement to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Oil prices rocket higher and stocks tumble after Trump’s address
video:video.video:description: Stocks slumped and oil prices soared Thursday after President Donald Trump vowed the U.S. will continue to attack Iran and failed to offer a clear timetable for ending the conflict in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: 'The Testaments' stars hope their show inspires like 'Handmaid's'
video:video.video:description: Hulu's "The Testaments" - starring Lucy Halliday and Chase Infiniti - is a sequel to hit dystopian drama "The Handmaid's Tale."

video:video.video:title: Rwanda tries to protect farmland in Africa’s most densely populated nation
video:video.video:description: Since September, Rwanda’s government has been mapping agricultural land and using satellite imagery to track any development encroaching on farmlands and forests in a country where the population is expected to reach 22 million in a couple of years.

video:video.video:title: Japan's Kabuki crown passes to a new generation
video:video.video:description: In Kabuki, Japan's traditional theater art dating back to the 1600s, passing down a name across generations is a sacred ritual.

video:video.video:title: Local affiliate of Iran state TV claims a US fighter pilot ejected from an aircraft in southwest Iran
video:video.video:description: The anchor on the channel read the following announcement: “If you capture the enemy pilot or pilots alive and hand them over to the police, you will receive a precious prize.”

video:video.video:title: Family of missing Navajo woman still waiting for answers
video:video.video:description: On June 15, 2021, Ella Mae Begay's truck was seen leaving her Sweetwater, Arizona home before dawn. Five years later, the missing woman's family is still seeking answers.

video:video.video:description: Two years ago, Dr. Mohammed Ziara watched Israel ravage Gaza's health care system, shelling hospitals, striking ambulances and forcing patients to evacuate.

video:video.video:description: Rescue teams worked to clear the debris from a damaged home in the central town of Ramat Gan on Saturday after Iran fired what the Israeli army said was a cluster munition towards Israel.

video:video.video:title: Al Jazeera journalist killed by Israeli drone strike in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Israel's military killed Al Jazeera correspondent Mohammed Wishah on Wednesday in a drone strike that targeted his car in the Gaza Strip, the media network and Palestinian health officials said.

video:video.video:title: Daniil Medvedev smashes racket seven times in 6-0, 6-0 defeat
video:video.video:description: Russia's Daniil Medvedev had a racket smashing moment in his 6-0, 6-0 defeat at Monte-Carlo Masters on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Polls close in Benin with finance minister favoured to succeed Talon as president
video:video.video:description: Polls closed in Benin on Sunday after voting to choose a successor to President Patrice Talon, who is stepping down after a decade in power, leaving a mixed legacy of economic growth, a growing jihadi insurgency in the north, and the suppression of opposition critics.

video:video.video:title: Russian strike damages port and energy infrastructure in Odesa
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian authorities said a drone attack overnight Thursday into Friday hit Odesa's port and energy infrastructure, damaging facilities and causing power disruptions.

video:video.video:title: A fast-growing wildfire in windy Southern California triggers evacuations
video:video.video:description: A smoky and fast-growing wildfire in Southern California has prompted multiple evacuation orders and warnings.

video:video.video:description: The arrival of spring has brought unusually high temperatures to Russia's capital, to the delight of its residents.

video:video.video:title: Brazil's Indigenous leaders rally as land disputes and mining pressures grow
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Indigenous people marched in Brazil’s capital on Tuesday to protest what they say are violations of their land rights by large corporations advancing farming, logging and mining projects.

video:video.video:description: The Center for Small Town Jewish Life at Colby College is growing as Passover ends this year. The center began a decade ago with a goal of supporting Jewish congregations that are far from big cities.

video:video.video:description: Offset, a former member of the influential hip-hop trio Migos, was shot outside a Florida casino and is in stable condition, a spokesperson said Tuesday.

video:video.video:description: A high-speed train driver was killed on Tuesday in northern France when his locomotive collided into a truck at a level crossing in Northern France.

video:video.video:description: A tanker truck in Texas hauling gasoline erupted into a fiery blaze on Sunday morning after colliding with another vehicle and knocking over power lines, leaving the truck driver in critical condition.

video:video.video:description: A stampede at a mountaintop fortress popular with tourists in northern Haiti has left at least 25 people dead and several dozen others injured.

video:video.video:description: Iranian-American Ali Khaleghi talked to his family in Tehran two weeks ago and said they were anxious, not because of the war going on in their country, but because the Iranian regime is still there.

video:video.video:title: Oil prices plunge and the Dow surges in a worldwide rally following tentative ceasefire with Iran
video:video.video:description: Oil prices are plunging back toward $95 per barrel, and stock markets are surging worldwide after President Donald Trump pulled back from his threat to force a whole civilization to die in the war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: The faith-based AI boom raises hopes and concerns
video:video.video:description: The faith-based AI market is expanding, with tools for various religions. As AI tools designed with religion in mind become increasingly common, many are reckoning with how these technologies shape their relationship to faith and spiritual guidance.

video:video.video:title: Lack of Hollywood movies in Cannes due to 'moment of transition'
video:video.video:description: Thierry Frémaux - the artistic director of the Cannes Film Festival - has said not to expect red carpet premieres like “Top Gun: Maverick” or “Mission: Impossible — The Final Reckoning” - both of which made splashy premieres in recent years - at the 2026 edition.

video:video.video:title: Oregon Zoo welcomes two penguin chicks
video:video.video:description: The Oregon Zoo welcomed two Humboldt penguin chicks that hatched about a week apart in March. Both are healthy and will nest with their parents until they are 3 months old.

video:video.video:title: Bangladesh conducts emergency measles vaccinations as outbreak kills more than 100 children
video:video.video:description: Bangladesh is conducting emergency measles-rubella vaccinations while trying to contain an ongoing outbreak that has killed more than 100 children in less than a month.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians in Gaza continue to face uncertainty six months after a fragile ceasefire took hold
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip continue to face uncertainty and fading hope six months after a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold.

video:video.video:title: 'I'm the space plumber' - astronaut Christina Koch fixes the toilet aboard Artemis II
video:video.video:description: One of the astronauts, Christina Koch, said there was an issue with the toilet but she acted as the plumber when the time came.

video:video.video:title: US retail sales rose more than expected in February
video:video.video:description: Shoppers increased their spending in February after pulling back at the start of the year, reflecting volatile and cautious activity by American consumers even before the Iran war sent gasoline prices soaring.

video:video.video:title: Horseback pilgrims in Chile celebrate the feast of Quasimodo
video:video.video:description: Some 2,000 Catholic pilgrims gathered in the Chilean city of Colina on Sunday for a traditional festival held on the first Sunday after Easter.

video:video.video:title: Apollo astronauts brought these items to the moon's surface
video:video.video:description: As Artemis II astronauts head to the moon for a fly-by, here are some Apollo items that have touched the lunar surface. (AP video/Shelby Lum)

video:video.video:title: Elle Fanning and Michelle Pfeiffer star in new comedy 'Margo's Got Money Troubles'
video:video.video:description: "Margo's Got Money Troubles" stars Elle Fanning as a college student who struggles to make ends meet after having a baby.

video:video.video:title: Barrage of strikes seen in Beirut after Israel vows to continue attacking Lebanon despite ceasefire
video:video.video:description: A barrage of strikes was seen across Beirut on Wednesday after Israel vowed to continue attacking Lebanon despite a ceasefire with Iran.

video:video.video:title: State TV reports on Iran president asking Americans in letter if war is in their interest
video:video.video:description: President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday issued a letter to the American people, asking them whether their government was really acting in their best interests.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s White House ballroom gets final approval days after a judge ordered a halt to construction
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump's new White House ballroom has gotten final approval from a key commission. This comes after a federal judge ordered a halt to construction unless Congress approves the project.

video:video.video:title: An unofficial ban keeps BTS at bay in China
video:video.video:description: BTS has launched their first world tour in over four years, but China is notably absent from the tour dates An unofficial ban has barred K-pop from the Chinese market, prompting entertainment companies to pivot toward Japan and North America.

video:video.video:title: Turkey is striving to bring about an end to the 'unlawful, meaningless' war in Iran, Erdogan says
video:video.video:description: Turkey’s President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said his country has intensified a push to end "the unlawful, meaningless" war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Meet the crew behind NASA's latest mission to the moon
video:video.video:description: NASA is preparing for its first crewed mission towards the moon in more than fifty years. The Artemis II mission will not land on the lunar surface, but it's a critical step in testing deep space travel and the crew reflects a new generation of international and more diverse human spaceflight.

video:video.video:title: Apple’s iPod is making a comeback
video:video.video:description: Secondhand sales of Apple iPods are surging four years after it was killed off, with young people showing a desire to listen to music in a focused way and with playlists not determined by algorithms.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu says Israel still has more objectives to complete in Iran despite ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Wednesday that there are "more objectives to complete" in Iran.

video:video.video:title: A priceless ancient golden helmet stolen from a Dutch museum is recovered
video:video.video:description: A priceless ancient golden helmet from Romania stolen last year from a museum in the Netherlands has been recovered, Dutch authorities announced Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Key commission set to vote on whether to approve Trump's new White House ballroom
video:video.video:description: A key commission is expected to vote on Thursday on whether to approve President Donald Trump's new White House ballroom.

video:video.video:title: Sierra Leone’s women rickshaw drivers are redefining who belongs behind the wheel
video:video.video:description: On the bustling streets of Freetown, Sierra Leone, women are taking the handlebars and breaking into rickshaw driving, a job long dominated by men.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter watches four-time champion Italy miss out on World Cup
video:video.video:description: Four-time champion Italy failed to qualify for a third straight World Cup after getting beat in a penalty shootout with 10 men at 66th-ranked Bosnia and Herzegovina in the European playoffs on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Benin president votes in country's election
video:video.video:description: Benin’s President, Patrice Talon, voted this Sunday at his polling place in Cotonou, the capital of Benin, as voters all over the country cast ballots to choose a successor to Talon, who is stepping down after a decade in power, leaving a mixed legacy of economic growth, a growing jihadi insurgency

video:video.video:title: Artemis crew can use the toilet as usual, NASA says, after reporting smell from bathroom
video:video.video:description: The toilet on Artemis II can be used as normal, entry flight director Rick Henfling said. It malfunctioned following Wednesday's liftoff and has been hit-or-miss ever since.

video:video.video:title: Huge fire burns in central Beirut after consecutive Israeli airstrikes hit the city
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes hit several dense commercial and residential areas in central Beirut without warning Wednesday, hours after a ceasefire was announced in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts rocket toward the moon after spending a day around Earth
video:video.video:description: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts have fired their engines and are blazing toward the moon. The so-called translunar ignition came 25 hours after liftoff, putting the three Americans and one Canadian on course for a lunar fly-around early next week.

video:video.video:title: Exclusive: Orbán challenger Magyar says election is a 'referendum' on Hungary's place in the world
video:video.video:description: Hungarian opposition leader Péter Magyar said Thursday that a crucial election next week will be a "referendum” on the country’s future.

video:video.video:title: Marilyn Monroe’s final signed check offers insight into her final day
video:video.video:description: A signed check by Marilyn Monroe is among items displayed ahead of an upcoming exhibit. Collector Bryan Johns of the Icon Collection says the document suggests Monroe was excited for the future on the day of her death.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 10
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, April 10th: Vice President JD Vance to lead mediated talks with Iran; Netanyahu authorizes direct Israel-Lebanon negotiations; Melania Trump denies knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein’s crimes; Artemis II crew to splash down on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Asia market jumps up on news of US and Iran agreeing to 2-week ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Oil prices plunged and U.S. stock futures jumped after U.S. President Donald Trump held off on his threat of devastating attacks on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Iran, the US and Israel agree to a temporary ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Iran, the United States and Israel reached a tentative, two-week ceasefire Wednesday in the war that tore across the Middle East and disrupted the global energy market, with U.S.

video:video.video:title: Don Cheadle, Ayo Edebiri set for inclusive Broadway debut in ‘Proof’
video:video.video:description: Don Cheadle and Ayo Edebiri reflect on the significance of their Broadway debuts in a revival of “Proof,” David Auburn’s Tony Award and Pulitzer Prize-winning play.

video:video.video:title: Elle Fanning had to explain Only Fans to Nicole Kidman for new show context
video:video.video:description: Elle Fanning had to explain Only Fans to Nicole Kidman for new show context

video:video.video:title: Iran marks 40-day mourning ceremony for slain supreme leader
video:video.video:description: In the capital Tehran, mourners wearing black began their rally from Jomhouri Eslami Square to the neighborhood of the office of Ali Khamenei in the heart of Tehran.

video:video.video:title: United Nations dispatching special envoy to Middle East
video:video.video:description: United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres says the spiral of death and destruction in the Middle East must stop.

video:video.video:title: Spring lambs audition for starring role in London's sheep drive
video:video.video:description: Spring lambs at a farm in the UK are "auditioning" for a starring role in London. The newborn lambs could soon be crossing one of London's busy bridges across the river Thames as part of the annual London Sheep Drive, a tradition dating back to medieval times.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters tackles flames after Russia strikes port infrastructure in Odesa
video:video.video:description: Russia has struck the Black Sea port city of Odesa with drones overnight, hitting civilian and port infrastructure, according to local authorities.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Offset shot and stable after incident at Seminole Hard Rock in Florida
video:video.video:description: The rapper Offset was shot in Florida, according to a spokesperson. The Seminole Police Department said a person sustained injuries that were not life-threatening Monday evening at a valet area at the Seminole Hard Rock in Hollywood, Florida.

video:video.video:title: Towering fountain of lava seen in Hawaii’s Kilauea Volcano
video:video.video:description: A tower of lava was visible, shooting up inside the Kilauea volcano on the Big Island of Hawaii on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Video shows gunfight between attackers and police outside Israeli Consulate in Istanbul
video:video.video:description: Video shows gunfight between attackers and police outside Israeli Consulate in Istanbul

video:video.video:title: Republican Clay Fuller wins election to U.S. House in Georgia’s 14th District
video:video.video:description: Republican Clay Fuller on Tuesday won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former U.S. House seat in Georgia, turning back a Democratic challenge with the help of President Donald Trump’s endorsement despite uneasiness over the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Earthquake off Indonesia topples buildings
video:video.video:description: The undersea magnitude 7.4 earthquake toppled buildings in parts of northern Indonesia, sent people fleeing from their homes, killed at least one person and generated a small tsunami Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Vance heads overseas for negotiations aimed at ending Iran war
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance is warning Iran not to “play” the U.S. as he heads for negotiations aimed at ending the war.

video:video.video:title: Chase Infiniti hopes 'The Testaments' will inspire Gen-Z to feel empowered and 'enact change'
video:video.video:description: Chase Infiniti stars in the highly anticipated "Handmaid's Tale" spin-off "The Testaments" and hopes the new series will inspire Gen Z to create change in their own communities.

video:video.video:title: Wall Street swings sharply as uncertainty increases about what will happen in the war with Iran
video:video.video:description: The S&P 500 fell as much as 1.2% Tuesday, hours ahead of a deadline by President Donald Trump to destroy Iranian power plants and bridges.

video:video.video:title: Gaudí confirmed as architect of unique mountain refuge
video:video.video:description: A long-running debate over a mountain chalet in Catalonia may finally have been settled. Officials now say the unusual building was designed by none other than Antoni Gaudí.

video:video.video:title: Strikes hit Beirut as Israel vows to continue attacking Lebanon despite Iran ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Israel's attacks came hours after a ceasefire was announced in the US-Israeli war with Iran. Israel has said the agreement does not extend to Lebanon, although mediator Pakistan said it does, and vowed to continue its ground invasion in the south.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong and other Asian markets retreat on concerns of fragile Middle East ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Oil rose again to above $97 a barrel and Asian stocks were trading lower Thursday on skepticism over a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese military withdraws from border towns as Israeli military pushes inwards
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese military said its forces have largely withdrawn from some remaining border towns as Israeli troops continue to push through southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday April 6th: U.S. and Iran received draft proposal for ceasefire; More strikes in Iran and Israel; Wounded U.S. aviator rescued; Artemis II to swing around Moon on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather for festival celebrating Japanese culture in Ohio
video:video.video:description: An organization dedicated to bringing together Ohio’s Japanese and American communities hosted a festival Sunday with a stage performances and other activities related to Japanese culture.

video:video.video:title: How worms are helping to create healthy soil for Kenyan farmers
video:video.video:description: Worms are helping improve soil on Kenyan farms. A venture just outside the capital uses the creatures to eat agricultural waste and animal dung, turning it into nutrient-rich compost.

video:video.video:title: Two dead and 14 injured in fireworks warehouse explosion in Russia
video:video.video:description: An explosion at a fireworks warehouse in the southern Russian city of Vladikavkaz killed at least two people and injured 14 others, including a baby, local authorities said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Pro-Iran government demonstrators burn US, Israeli flags in Tehran following news of ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Pro-government demonstrators gathered and chanted in the streets of Iran’s capital Wednesday morning after the ceasefire was announced. (AP video shot by Mohsen Ganji)

video:video.video:title: Cherry blossom trees falling triggers safety concern as Japan marks the season
video:video.video:description: Many of Tokyo's popular and iconic Somei Yoshino cherry blossom trees were planted during Japan's postwar advancement in the 1960s, and are now getting old and frail.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV remembers his predecessor in Easter Monday blessing
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV remembered his predecessor Pope Francis’s "profound witness of faith and love" during the traditional Easter Monday blessing.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Pooh Shiesty charged with kidnapping over alleged dispute involving rapper Gucci Mane's label
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors in Texas accuse rapper Pooh Shiesty and eight others of robbing three men at gunpoint and kidnapping them following a contract dispute in January involving rapper Gucci Mane’s record label

video:video.video:title: South Africans react to ‘Lion King’ composer suing comedian
video:video.video:description: A Grammy-award winning South African composer who wrote and performed the iconic opening chant in “Circle of Life” for Disney's “The Lion King" movies is suing a comedian for allegedly damaging his reputation by intentionally misrepresenting the song's meaning.

video:video.video:title: Ringo Starr on seeing Beatles bandmate Paul McCartney in concert
video:video.video:description: Ringo Starr reflects on watching Paul McCartney perform an intimate show at the Los Angeles Fonda Theatre in March.

video:video.video:title: Presidential candidates vote in election where 35 contenders are vying for ninth president in 10 years
video:video.video:description: Presidential candidates voted on Sunday in Lima as the country makes their pick in the general election for next president from an unusually large field of 35 candidates, amid concerns about crime, corruption, and the country’s persistent political instability.

video:video.video:title: Russia and China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz as US deadline for Iran deal looms
video:video.video:description: Russia and China on Tuesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz that had been repeatedly watered down in hopes those two countries would abstain.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians mourn Al Jazeera journalist Mohammad Wishah targeted by Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Palestinians gathered Thursday morning at Al-Aqsa hospital in central Gaza to bid a final farewell to Al Jazeera correspondent Mohammed Wishah, who was killed in an Israeli drone strike the day before.

video:video.video:title: Nutella jar seen spinning through Artemis II
video:video.video:description: Minutes before the Artemis II mission shattered the distance record set by Apollo 13 in 1970 a jar of Nutella hazelnut spread was seen spinning through the spacecraft.

video:video.video:title: Michigan fans gather outside basketball arena in Ann Arbor to welcome the 2026 national champs home
video:video.video:description: Michigan fans gathered outside Crisler Center to welcome the freshly crowned 2026 men’s basketball national champions back to Ann Arbor (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Funeral of 13 Lebanese service officers killed in Israeli strike on southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Thirteen members of Lebanon’s State Security Service were laid to rest on Saturday in Sidon, a day after being killed in an Israeli strike in the southern city of Nabatieh.

video:video.video:title: Fans react to opening night of BTS' world tour
video:video.video:description: Fans react to BTS’ return to the stage after nearly four years, at the opening night of the K-pop megastars' "ARIRANG" world tour.

video:video.video:title: Iran to take 'serious legal steps' to protect its cultural sites
video:video.video:description: Iranian Minister of Cultural Heritage, Tourism and Handicrafts Sayed Reza Salihi-Amiri said on Sunday that Iran would take "serious legal steps" to protect its cultural sites, after Israeli and U.S. bombardments have damaged over 100 sites across the country.

video:video.video:title: 'Ketamine Queen' who supplied drugs to Matthew Perry set for sentencing
video:video.video:description: A woman who sold actor Matthew Perry the drug ketamine that killed him is set to be sentenced Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Tension remain high at Israel-Lebanon border as Hezbollah attacks continue to set off sirens
video:video.video:description: Tensions remained high on the Israeli-Lebanese border as rocket and drone attacks by the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah continued to set off sirens throughout the day on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Baby camel born in UK zoo
video:video.video:description: Sophia was born to Bactrian camels mum Izzy and dad Oakley at Whipsnade Zoo in Bedfordshire, boosting the herd’s total to eight.

video:video.video:title: Measles threatens babies in South Carolina and the nation as community vaccination rates decline
video:video.video:description: Babies too young to be vaccinated are among the most vulnerable in measles outbreaks like the one in South Carolina, which is the largest the U.S. has seen in decades.

video:video.video:title: 1 killed, 2 presumed dead after partial roof collapse at parking garage being built in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a stairwell roof at a parking garage being built in Philadelphia collapsed suddenly, killing one person and leaving two missing and presumed dead.

video:video.video:title: Trump says it would take 4 hours to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump described the consequences that Iran would face if it didn’t reach a deal with the U.S. by Trump’s 8 p.m. Eastern Tuesday deadline.

video:video.video:title: Blind Massachusetts man is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets
video:video.video:description: Matthew Shifrin is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets using free accessibility instructions created by sighted and blind volunteers in his nonprofit group.

video:video.video:title: Pressure mounts on Kanye West to be pulled from headline role at summer music festival in London
video:video.video:description: Pressure was mounting Sunday on the American rapper Kanye West to be pulled from his headline role at a London music festival this summer.

video:video.video:title: Price hikes hit Greeks shopping in preparation for Orthodox Easter celebration
video:video.video:description: Price increases due to the US-Israel war on Iran have hit Greek shoppers as they prepare to celebrate Orthodox Easter on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II’s astronauts return to Earth with a splashdown in the Pacific
video:video.video:description: The three Americans and one Canadian have returned with a dramatic splashdown Friday evening, as their capsule parachuted into the Pacific to close out a nearly 10-day trip to the moon and back.

video:video.video:title: 'Malcolm in the Middle' cast returns for more chaos
video:video.video:description: Bryan Cranston, Frankie Muniz, and Jane Kaczmarek reunited Tuesday in New York to celebrate the new Hulu limited series “Malcolm in the Middle: Life’s Still Unfair."

video:video.video:title: Jon Hamm compares working on 'The Morning Show' with 'Your Friends and Neighbors'
video:video.video:description: Jon Hamm compares the “murderer’s row” of talent on "The Morning Show" to the Broadway-caliber lineup on "Your Friends and Neighbors." (April 15)
